treatment_information.treatment_response: Histologic sections showed a high-grade hydronephrosis, pyelonephritis, and fibrosis without evidence of malignancy. Postoperatively, the CA 19-9 serum level decreased at a rate of approximately 100 to 200 U/mL per day.

treatment_timeline.event: Follow-up examination 11 years after breast cancer surgery revealed an elevated CA 19-9 serum level (3049 U/mL).
Subsequent staging procedures showed high-grade, right-sided hydronephrosis.
Open transperitoneal right-sided nephrectomy was performed.
Postoperatively, the CA 19-9 serum level decreased at a rate of approximately 100 to 200 U/mL per day.
The patient was well and the CA 19-9 value was within the normal range (17 U/mL).

patient_outcome.final_status: Well with CA 19-9 value within the normal range (17 U/mL)
